resources.string: Lets you lock the screen with a double-tap, for phones where the power button doesn\'t work.
Only detect taps on home/lock screen
Disable gesture while camera is open
Tap the box below %1$d times quickly to test lock detection
Would lock the screen now (demo only \u2014 nothing actually locked)
MIUI stops background apps aggressively. Tap below, then enable autostart for this app.
ColorOS restricts background apps. Tap below, then allow this app to run in the background.
Funtouch OS restricts background apps. Tap below, then enable background/autostart access.
EMUI/Magic UI restricts background apps. Tap below, enable \'Manage manually\', then turn on Auto-launch.
Tap below, then enable auto-start for this app.
Your device (%1$s) doesn\'t usually need extra setup beyond the battery optimization exemption from Setup.
Couldn\'t find manufacturer settings \u2014 opening app info instead
This app uses Device Admin permission, which blocks normal uninstall. Tap below to safely release that permission first, then continue to uninstall.
So the app can tell when you tap the screen to lock it, Android needs Accessibility access. This only watches for tap patterns \u2014 it does not read or record your screen.
This lets the floating volume button appear on top of whatever you\'re doing, so you can change the volume without opening the app.
This helps the app know when you\'re on the home or lock screen, so tap-to-sleep only triggers there \u2014 not while you\'re using other apps.
Device Admin access lets the app actually lock your screen when you tap. Without it, tap-to-sleep can\'t work.
Phones try to save battery by stopping apps you\'re not looking at. This exemption keeps shake-to-wake working even after the screen\'s been off for a while.
To let the volume bubble switch Ring and Notification volume down to silent or vibrate, Android requires Do Not Disturb access.
Needed to show the persistent volume control notification with quick +/- buttons.
